idea-plugin.name: Pre-Push Compilation Checker
idea-plugin.description:
Blocks git pushes when your code has compilation errors — before they reach the remote.

How it works

  • IDE Push Guard: Hooks into the IntelliJ Git Push dialog via the prePushHandler extension point. Checks whether the IDE already knows about problems in the pushed files, then compiles the affected modules and their dependents. A full project build is used only when build files or file deletions are involved.
  • Strict A/B preflight: When enabled, the plugin escalates only risky pushes to exact pushed-snapshot validation, so local-only dependent changes cannot silently hide a compile failure in the pushed commits. A symbolic check (git diff HEAD of unpushed local files vs. HEAD content of pushed files) catches A/B mismatches in milliseconds without invoking a build.
  • Auto-retry on success / choice dialog on failure: When the background pre-push check passes, the plugin runs git push automatically per repository root. When errors are found, a modal dialog offers Reset Commit / Push Anyway / Leave Commit / Cancel.
  • Terminal Push Guard: On project open, the plugin installs a managed pre-push Git hook that reuses the running IDE compiler when available, then falls back to a fast Gradle or Maven compile before allowing any push from a terminal or external git client. The fallback path stashes the working tree first so the build sees only HEAD content and A/B mismatches still surface.

Important: The plugin is a local preflight. Required Merge Request CI with branch protection should remain the authoritative gate before changes reach main/master.

Features

  • Smart compile scope — changed modules plus dependent modules by default, project-level when necessary
  • Debounced warmup compiles — keeps the IDE compiler cache hot for near-instant pushes
  • IDE problem check — skips compilation if IntelliJ already reports errors
  • Navigable error dialog — double-click any error to jump to the source file
  • Refresh action — re-run the check from the block dialog without cancelling the push
  • Compilation Checker tool window — side panel showing errors with file navigation
  • Gradle (wrapper or system) and Maven (wrapper or system) support
  • Optional stash fallback for strict A/B validation when detached worktrees are unavailable
  • Respects PRE_PUSH_CHECKER_COMMAND env variable for custom check commands

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2023.3 or later
  • Java 17+
  • Java or Kotlin (JVM) project
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.5.2

  • First-time warmup compile: on the first project open after plugin install, a full background compile now runs automatically once indexing finishes. This primes the compiler cache so the first push from a terminal or GUI git client (Sublime Merge, SourceTree, GitHub Desktop) immediately reuses IntelliJ's incremental result instead of falling back to Maven/Gradle with a cold cache. Subsequent opens are unaffected.

1.5.1

  • External push race fix (Sublime Merge / terminal): the managed hook now waits longer for IntelliJ's local compiler socket before falling back to Maven/Gradle, preventing first-attempt false failures that succeeded on immediate retry.
  • Generated-symbol fallback rescue: when build-tool fallback reports getter/setter-style generated symbol errors (get*, set*, is*) and IntelliJ is running, the hook retries IntelliJ incremental compile once before aborting the push.
  • Explicit fallback diagnostics: hook logs now include the reason IntelliJ incremental compile was unavailable, so external push failures are easier to debug from .idea/pre-push-checker/last-run.log.
  • Proactive uninstall cleanup: the plugin now tracks managed repos and cleans their managed hook/cache/exclude artifacts during uninstall, including repos that are not currently open in the IDE.

1.5.0

  • Self-cleanup on uninstall: when the plugin is uninstalled, a global marker (~/.prepush-checker/installed) is removed. Orphaned hooks in repos that were not open during uninstall now detect the missing marker on the next push and automatically remove themselves, the managed hook script, the cache directory, and the .git/info/exclude block. No manual cleanup needed.
  • Push Anyway (IDE): the compilation error dialog now includes a Push Anyway button in both the synchronous (cached errors, IDE problems) and background compilation paths. A one-shot bypass token is written so the git hook also allows the push through.
  • Force Next Push (terminal / Sublime Merge / external clients): a new toggle action in the Compilation Checker toolbar lets users skip the compilation check for the next push attempt. The bypass is single-use (consumed on the next push) and expires after 1 hour to prevent stale flags.
  • Settings panel reorganized: checkboxes are now grouped under a titled “Settings” border for a cleaner, more organized look.

1.4.6

  • External hook now waits for a running IntelliJ before fallback: when the first IDE socket probe fails, the managed pre-push hook now detects an active IntelliJ process and retries the IDE compile request for a short window before dropping to Gradle/Maven fallback. This reduces false fallback usage when the IDE is running in the background and the loopback service is still initializing.
  • Fallback compile cache for unchanged HEAD: when IntelliJ is unavailable and the hook uses build-tool fallback, successful results are now reused for identical HEAD + compile mode (main/test/all). Repeated pushes without new commits avoid redundant backend compiles while preserving existing failure behavior.

1.4.5

  • External IntelliJ fast-path false positives fixed: terminal, Sublime Merge, and other external git clients no longer block pushes from stale IntelliJ editor problem-cache entries. The loopback server now reuses only cached successful compile verdicts; cached failures are rechecked with CompilerManager.make before the hook reports an error. This prevents stale generated-symbol failures for Lombok-style getters, setters, and builders from persisting until the user manually runs the IntelliJ compilation check. If the external fast-path's scoped make still reports errors, it now retries once with the same project-scope make used by the tool-window compilation check before blocking the push.

1.4.4

  • Lombok ghost errors in the Maven fallback fixed: when the IDE socket is unreachable and the hook (or the in-IDE strict snapshot guard) falls back to mvn / ./mvnw, Maven's incremental compiler could re-compile a consumer against a stale target/classes copy of an @Getter / @Setter class — emitting false cannot find symbol errors (e.g. setCloseIcon, setFromSheetView, isFromSheetView) immediately after a merge that introduced new Lombok-generated members. Both Maven invocation sites now pass -Dmaven.compiler.useIncrementalCompilation=false so annotation processing always sees fresh sources. The IDE fast path (PrePushLocalServer → CompilerManager.make) was unaffected and is still used whenever IntelliJ is running.

1.4.3

  • GUI git client PATH reconstruction: the managed hook now sources the user's shell rc files (.zshenv, .zprofile, .zshrc, .bash_profile, .bashrc, .profile) and appends known install locations (/opt/homebrew/bin, SDKMAN, jenv, asdf, $MAVEN_HOME/bin, $GRADLE_HOME/bin) so that mvn and gradle resolve correctly when the hook is launched from Sublime Merge, SourceTree, GitHub Desktop, or any other GUI client that strips the user's interactive PATH. No effect on repos that ship a ./mvnw or ./gradlew wrapper.

1.4.2

  • IntelliJ 2026.1 fix: All four projectActivity entrypoints (managed pre-push hook installer, external hook log loader, local TCP server, warmup service) are now Kotlin ProjectActivity implementations that delegate to the existing Java logic. IntelliJ 2026.1 silently stopped invoking Java ProjectActivity implementations, which caused the local TCP server to never start — terminal / Sublime Merge / SourceTree pushes fell through to the raw Maven/Gradle fallback (and surfaced false "cannot find symbol" errors in projects whose Maven build relies on the IDE for Lombok-style annotation processing). After upgrading and restarting the IDE, .idea/pre-push-checker/server.port is written again and external pushes reuse IntelliJ's incremental compiler.

1.4.1

  • Marketplace compatibility cleanup: removed deprecated PerformInBackgroundOption usage from background pre-push and auto-retry tasks while preserving always-background behavior. Plugin Verifier for IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1.1 now reports the plugin as compatible without deprecated API warnings.

1.4.0

  • Symbolic A/B detection: the strict guard now scans git diff HEAD of unpushed local files for newly added method/class/field declarations and checks HEAD content of pushed files for word-boundary references. Catches the canonical A/B case (push references a symbol defined only in an unpushed local edit) instantly, without invoking a build.
  • macOS path normalization: snapshot output now strips both /var/folders/... and /private/var/folders/... forms so filterSnapshotErrors retains errors actually in pushed files.
  • Auto-retry push on success: when the background pre-push check passes, the plugin runs git push per repository root with non-interactive credentials (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0), /dev/null stdin, and a 120s timeout. Per-push elapsed-ms is logged to idea.log.
  • Failure-choice dialog: when errors are found, a modal dialog offers Reset Commit (soft-reset the pushed commits, keep changes), Push Anyway, Leave Commit, or Cancel.
  • HEAD-snapshot stash in the external pre-push hook fallback: when the IDE socket is unreachable, the hook stashes the working tree before running Gradle/Maven so the build sees only HEAD content; an EXIT INT TERM HUP trap guarantees stash pop on any interrupt. Closes the A/B coverage gap when the IDE is closed and pushes happen via terminal / Sublime Merge / GitHub Desktop.

1.3.1

  • Risk-scoped strict A/B dependency guard: the side-panel toggle now escalates only when local source/build changes can plausibly mask pushed-snapshot failures. Risky pushes are validated against committed HEAD in a clean temporary worktree; an explicit advanced stash fallback is available when worktrees cannot be created.
  • CI remains authoritative: required Merge Request CI and branch protection are documented as the non-bypassable safety net before main/master accepts changes.

1.3.0

  • External pushes reuse IntelliJ's incremental compiler: terminal and GUI-client pushes now contact a local loopback server when the IDE is open, then fall back to Gradle or Maven if IntelliJ is unavailable.
  • Zero-cost warmup path: debounced background compiles after source saves keep compiler caches hot so most pre-push checks reuse a fresh verdict.
  • Stronger dependency coverage: targeted compile scopes now include dependent modules, catching caller-side breakage without forcing full project builds for ordinary source changes.
  • Bounded error retention: compiler errors and external hook log entries are compacted, deduplicated, and truncated where needed to keep memory usage predictable.
  • Repository cleanliness: plugin-owned hook/log/cache paths are excluded via repo-local .git/info/exclude and cleaned up when the plugin is disabled or uninstalled.

1.2.0

  • External push guard (terminal, Sublime Merge, SourceTree, GitHub Desktop, etc.): hook installation now resolves the real hooks directory via git rev-parse --git-path hooks, so it works in worktrees, submodules, and repos that set core.hooksPath.
  • Stricter external check: the hook now also compiles test sources (classes testClasses / test-compile) so test-only breakage is caught.
  • Errors from external pushes surface inside IntelliJ: the hook writes a log to .idea/pre-push-checker/last-run.log. A new startup activity parses that log on project open and on every hook run, feeds errors into the Compilation Checker tool window, and raises a notification with an "Open Compilation Checker" action.
  • Managed wrapper is refreshed on every project open so hook upgrades ship automatically.

1.1.0

  • Tool window: Replaced the "Clear" action with a "Report Issue" action that opens the plugin's GitHub Issues page with a pre-populated, concise title (no large error payload stuffed into the URL).
  • Readable error list: Entries now render as FileName:line:col — message instead of the raw [full/deep/path (line, col)] message. The full entry is preserved as a tooltip on hover.
  • Navigation fix: Double-click / Enter now correctly opens the source file for entries that use the [path (line, col)] compiler format. Also supports paths containing spaces.
  • Pre-push EDT fix: Replaced ModalityState.any() with the progress indicator's modality (falling back to the default) when triggering a compilation from a background thread. Prevents "Write-unsafe context" errors during compile setup.
  • Robustness: Null-safe compiler-message formatting, exception-isolated listener fan-out in CompilationErrorService, and short-circuit when the new error list equals the previous snapshot.
  • Performance: Per-extension icon cache in the list renderer, allocation-free source-extension check in PushValidationPaths, reused StringBuilder when formatting compiler messages, and pre-sized result lists.

1.0.1

  • Fix EDT violation when triggering a compilation check from the tool window Run Check button

1.0.0 — Initial Release

  • Pre-push handler for the IntelliJ Git Push dialog
  • Smart module-level compile scope with automatic full-build fallback
  • IDE problem check using WolfTheProblemSolver before triggering a build
  • Compilation error dialog with file-type icons, navigation, and Refresh action
  • Compilation Checker tool window (right side panel)
  • Git hook installer for terminal push support (Gradle & Maven)
  • CompilationErrorService for cross-component error state sharing

registryKey.description: Run a debounced background compile of recently saved files so pre-push checks hit the cache and finish instantly.

Maximum number of modules (pushed modules plus their dependents) included in the pre-push compile scope. If exceeded, falls back to file scope to avoid churning the world when a widely-used utility module is pushed.
